messages.title: Word Nerd Alert ◈
messages.body: Did you know? The word 'set' has the most definitions of any English word - over 430!

messages.title: Quick Brain Teaser ⬡
messages.body: What 5-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters? Answer: Short!

messages.title: Plummet Wisdom ◐
messages.body: The longest word you can type using only the top row of a keyboard is 'typewriter'!

messages.title: Time for Wordplay ▣
messages.body: Your letters miss you. They've been sitting there spelling nothing without you.

messages.title: Fun Fact ◇
messages.body: 'Dreamt' is the only English word ending in 'mt'. Sweet dreams!

messages.title: Letter Lovers Unite ⊕
messages.body: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. (That's every letter! Can you beat that?)

messages.title: Daily Giggle ◎
messages.body: Why did the word go to therapy? It had too many issues with its past tense.

messages.title: Mind Blown ✦
messages.body: 'Underground' and 'underfund' are the only English words starting and ending with 'und'!

messages.title: Plummet Pun ◉
messages.body: I told my friend a word joke. It was a play on words... he didn't get the letter of it.

messages.title: Random Wisdom ⬢
messages.body: 'Bookkeeper' and 'bookkeeping' have three consecutive double letters. Try finding another!

messages.title: Grammar Giggles ◆
messages.body: The past, present, and future walked into a bar. It was tense.

messages.title: Word Wizard Tip ✧
messages.body: Pro tip: Words with Q but no U exist! Try QI, QOPH, or QADI in your next game.

messages.title: Language Laughs ⊙
messages.body: Why do words never argue? Because it would be a vowel dispute!

messages.title: Today's Challenge ▲
messages.body: Can you think of a word with all 5 vowels in order? (Hint: facetious!)

messages.title: Nerdy Knowledge ⬣
messages.body: 'Stewardesses' is the longest word typed with only the left hand on a QWERTY keyboard!

messages.title: Alphabet Soup ◌
messages.body: The letter E appears in 11% of all English words. E's are everywhere!

messages.title: Daily Dad Joke ⊞
messages.body: I used to be a banker but I lost interest. Now I just spell words for fun!

messages.title: Word Trivia ⧫
messages.body: 'Rhythms' is the longest word without a vowel. Try using it in Plummet!

messages.title: Linguist Laughs ◑
messages.body: An anagram of 'astronomer' is 'moon starer'. The universe has jokes!

messages.title: Quick Quiz ▽
messages.body: Which day is best for spelling? Sunday - because SUNday has the word SUN!

messages.title: Plummet Calling ◈
messages.body: Your high score is lonely. It's been waiting for company. Come beat it!

messages.title: Dictionary Doozy ⬡
messages.body: 'Strengths' is the longest word with just one vowel. Strong word choice!

messages.title: Letter Logic ⊡
messages.body: The word 'queue' is just Q followed by 4 silent letters demanding attention.

messages.title: Wordy Wisdom ◐
messages.body: If you rearrange 'LISTEN' you get 'SILENT'. Mind = blown.

messages.title: Game Time ▣
messages.body: Studies show playing word games makes you 100% more awesome. Source: us.

messages.title: Puzzle Break ◇
messages.body: What English word has three consecutive double letters? Hint: it keeps track of money!

messages.title: Word Nerd News ⊕
messages.body: 'Uncopyrightable' is the longest word with no repeated letters. Try spelling that!

messages.title: Vocabulary Vibes ◎
messages.body: Today's mood: Sesquipedalian (a person who uses long words). Be fancy!

messages.title: Brain Food ✦
messages.body: Playing word games is basically a salad for your brain. Very nutritious!

messages.title: Spelling Bee ◉
messages.body: Bees don't have time for spelling. But you do! Buzz on over to Plummet.

messages.title: Letter Love ⬢
messages.body: Roses are red, violets are blue, I'm running out of letters, how about you?

messages.title: Random Riddle ◆
messages.body: I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. What am I? An echo! Now go play!

messages.title: Linguistic Luck ✧
messages.body: 'Forty' is the only number spelled with letters in alphabetical order!

messages.title: Word of Encouragement ⊙
messages.body: You know what's a great word? YOU. Because YOU are great at word games!

messages.title: Letter Alert ▲
messages.body: Breaking news: Letters spotted forming words without supervision. Please investigate!

messages.title: Punctuation Pun ⬣
messages.body: Never date an apostrophe. They're too possessive!

messages.title: Vowel Movement ◌
messages.body: A, E, I, O, U... and sometimes Y. Y though? Y is it like this?

messages.title: Plummet Fact ⊞
messages.body: Average Plummet player: 3% word genius, 97% determined. You've got this!

messages.title: Dictionary Detective ⧫
messages.body: 'Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis' is the longest word in the dictionary. Don't worry, we won't use it!

messages.title: Wordsmith Wink ◑
messages.body: Why did the dictionary break up with the thesaurus? It wanted to see other meanings!

messages.title: Letter Limerick ▽
messages.body: There once was a letter named Z, who felt left out frequently. But in Plummet's game, it rose to fame, and spelled its way to victory!

messages.title: Morning Motivation ◈
messages.body: Every word you spell is a tiny victory. Go collect some victories today!

messages.title: Afternoon Amusement ⬡
messages.body: The afternoon slump? Word games are scientifically proven* to help! (*Not actually scientific)

messages.title: Evening Entertainment ⊡
messages.body: Netflix or word games? That's basically the same thing for your brain. We checked.

messages.title: Silly Synonym ◐
messages.body: What do you call a dinosaur that knows a lot of words? A Thesaurus!

messages.title: Alphabet Appreciation ▣
messages.body: Shoutout to A-Z for always being there in the right order. Real ones!

messages.title: Word Workout ◇
messages.body: Your brain called. It's tired of watching TV and wants to spell things. Help it out!

messages.title: Friendly Reminder ⊕
messages.body: This is your daily reminder that you're smarter than you think. Now prove it!

messages.title: Game Facts ◎
messages.body: Fun fact: Playing Plummet increases your awesomeness by at least 47%. Trust us.

messages.title: Consonant Comedy ✦
messages.body: Consonants: 'We do all the work!' Vowels: 'At least we're not always silent...'

messages.title: Etymology Edge ◉
messages.body: The word 'etymology' comes from Greek. Even studying word origins has word origins!

messages.title: Pattern Recognition ⬢
messages.body: Your brain is literally a pattern machine. Word games are just fuel for the machine.

messages.title: Silent Letters ◆
messages.body: The K in 'knight' is like that friend who shows up but never says anything.

messages.title: Wordplay Workout ✧
messages.body: Anagram of 'dormitory'? 'Dirty room.' Your brain just did a push-up.

messages.title: Letter Frequency ⊙
messages.body: The letter Z appears in only 0.074% of English words. It's basically a celebrity.

messages.title: Prefix Power ▲
messages.body: 'Un-' is the most common prefix in English. Unfortunately, that's undeniably true.

messages.title: Suffix Stats ⬣
messages.body: '-ness' turns adjectives into nouns. That's the coolness of suffixes for you.

messages.title: Compound Interest ◌
messages.body: 'Breakfast' = break + fast. You're literally breaking your overnight fast. Mind blown?

messages.title: Homophone Humor ⊞
messages.body: I wood write more would jokes, but eye can't bare it. Neither can ewe.

messages.title: Palindrome Party ⧫
messages.body: 'A man, a plan, a canal: Panama!' reads the same forwards and backwards. Magic.

messages.title: Onomatopoeia Hour ◑
messages.body: Buzz, hiss, splash, bang! Some words just sound like what they mean. How fitting.

messages.title: Root Word Reveal ▽
messages.body: 'Telephone' = tele (far) + phone (sound). You're speaking far-sound. Ancient Greek is cool.

messages.title: Antonym Awareness ◈
messages.body: Hot and cold. Big and small. Playing Plummet and being bored. Opposites exist!

messages.title: Synonym Suggestion ⬡
messages.body: Tired of 'good'? Try splendid, stellar, superb, or sensational. Variety is the spice of words.

messages.title: Contraction Action ⊡
messages.body: Can't, won't, shouldn't... Contractions are just words being lazy. We respect that.

messages.title: Vowel Value ◐
messages.body: Without vowels, 'Plummet' would be 'Plmmt.' Hard to pronounce. Vowels matter!

messages.title: Consonant Cluster ▣
messages.body: 'Strengths' has the longest consonant cluster in common English words. Six consonants!

messages.title: Digraph Discovery ◇
messages.body: 'Ch', 'sh', 'th' - two letters, one sound. English is basically a puzzle game itself.

messages.title: Blend Basics ⊕
messages.body: 'Brunch' = breakfast + lunch. 'Motel' = motor + hotel. Portmanteaus are word math.

messages.title: Acronym Alert ◎
messages.body: SCUBA = Self-Contained Underwater Breathing Apparatus. Every acronym tells a story.

messages.title: Initialism Insight ✦
messages.body: FBI, CIA, LOL - some letter combos are just too important to spell out.

messages.title: Eponym Education ◉
messages.body: Sandwiches are named after the Earl of Sandwich. History is delicious.

messages.title: Borrowed Words ⬢
messages.body: 'Karaoke' is Japanese, 'Safari' is Swahili, 'Pizza' is Italian. English is a collector.

messages.title: Neologism News ◆
messages.body: New words enter the dictionary every year. 'Selfie' wasn't a word until 2013!

messages.title: Archaic Appeal ✧
messages.body: 'Forsooth' and 'wherein' may be old, but they're still in the dictionary. Respect your elders.

messages.title: Slang Status ⊙
messages.body: Yesterday's slang is today's standard. 'Cool' used to be controversial. Now it's just... cool.

messages.title: Jargon Junction ▲
messages.body: Every field has its own words. In Plummet, we call that 'vocabulary diversification.'

messages.title: Idiom Insight ⬣
messages.body: 'Break a leg' means good luck. Languages are weird. We love it.

messages.title: Proverb Prompt ◌
messages.body: The early bird catches the worm, but the second mouse gets the cheese. Choose wisely.

messages.title: Metaphor Moment ⊞
messages.body: Life is a journey. Word games are the scenic route. Enjoy the view.

messages.title: Simile Suggestion ⧫
messages.body: You're as sharp as a tack when you play Plummet. Like, literally.

messages.title: Hyperbole Hour ◑
messages.body: We've told you a million times: hyperbole is the best thing ever in the history of forever.

messages.title: Oxymoron Observation ▽
messages.body: 'Jumbo shrimp' and 'act naturally' - some word pairs just want to cause trouble.

messages.title: Alliteration Appreciation ◈
messages.body: Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers. Tongue twisters are basically word gymnastics.

messages.title: Assonance Awareness ⬡
messages.body: The rain in Spain stays mainly in the plain. Vowel sounds can be so satisfying.

messages.title: Rhyme Time ⊡
messages.body: Cat, hat, bat, mat. Rhyming is basically word matching. You're already a pro at that.

messages.title: Rhythm & Words ◐
messages.body: Words have natural beats. 'Plummet' is a trochee: PLUM-met. Now you know poetry.

messages.title: Syntax Snippet ▣
messages.body: Word order matters. 'Dog bites man' vs 'Man bites dog.' Same words, different headlines.

messages.title: Semantics Session ◇
messages.body: Meaning is everything. 'I love word games' means something different when you're crying.

messages.title: Pragmatics Point ⊕
messages.body: 'Can you pass the salt?' isn't really a question. It's a very polite command.

messages.title: Morpheme Memo ◎
messages.body: 'Unhappiness' has three morphemes: un + happy + ness. Words are made of smaller words!

messages.title: Phoneme Fact ✦
messages.body: English has about 44 distinct sounds. You mastered them all before kindergarten.

messages.title: Grapheme Guide ◉
messages.body: The sound /k/ can be spelled: c, k, ck, ch, q, que. English spelling is an adventure.

messages.title: Lexicon Lesson ⬢
messages.body: Your mental dictionary has somewhere between 20,000-35,000 words. That's a lot of pages!

messages.title: Grammar Gem ◆
messages.body: Adjective order: opinion-size-age-shape-color-origin-material-purpose. 'Big old green Irish wooden rocking chair.'

messages.title: Punctuation Point ✧
messages.body: The Oxford comma: 'I love my parents, Batman, and Superman' vs '...my parents, Batman and Superman.'

messages.title: Capitalization Cue ⊙
messages.body: Proper nouns get capitals. That's why it's 'Plummet' and not 'plummet.' We're proper.

messages.title: Spelling Surprise ▲
messages.body: 'Colonel' is pronounced 'kernel.' English spelling had one job and just... didn't do it.

messages.title: Pronunciation Puzzle ⬣
messages.body: 'Ghoti' can be pronounced 'fish' (gh as in enough, o as in women, ti as in nation). Thanks, English.

messages.title: Dialect Delight ◌
messages.body: Color or colour? Realize or realise? The Atlantic Ocean divides more than just continents.

messages.title: Accent Appreciation ⊞
messages.body: The same word sounds different everywhere. That's not a bug, it's a feature.

messages.title: Register Reality ⧫
messages.body: 'Hey!' vs 'Good evening' - context changes everything. Words are chameleons.

messages.title: Tone Talk ◑
messages.body: In text, 'Fine.' can mean anything from 'great' to 'I will remember this.' Tone matters.

messages.title: Context Clue ▽
messages.body: 'Bank' could be river or money. Context is the detective that solves the mystery.

messages.title: Ambiguity Alert ◈
messages.body: 'Flying planes can be dangerous.' The planes? The act of flying them? Yes.

messages.title: Clarity Call ⬡
messages.body: Clear writing is clear thinking. If you can spell it, you can think it.

messages.title: Concision Cue ⊡
messages.body: Why use many word when few word do trick? Brevity is the soul of wit.

messages.title: Vocabulary Victory ◐
messages.body: Learning a new word is like finding money in your pocket. Free mental wealth!

messages.title: Memory Magic ▣
messages.body: Your brain stores words in networks. 'Dog' connects to 'pet' to 'cat' to 'fur.' It's all linked.

messages.title: Learning Loop ◇
messages.body: You need to see a word 6-17 times to remember it. Good thing Plummet has lots of words!

messages.title: Practice Perfect ⊕
messages.body: Word games are vocabulary practice in disguise. Shh, don't tell your brain it's learning.

messages.title: Challenge Check ◎
messages.body: Your brain grows when challenged. Consider this your daily brain gym session.

messages.title: Focus Feature ✦
messages.body: Word games require attention. That's focus training without the meditation cushion.

messages.title: Speed Skill ◉
messages.body: The faster you recognize patterns, the faster you find words. You're training speed-reading!

messages.title: Pattern Perception ⬢
messages.body: Finding words in a grid? That's pattern recognition. Your brain is basically doing pushups.

messages.title: Spatial Sense ◆
messages.body: Words can go in any direction. Your spatial reasoning just signed up for a workout.

messages.title: Sequential Skill ✧
messages.body: Letters must be in order. Understanding sequences is fundamental to everything. Thanks, words!

messages.title: Cognitive Cardio ⊙
messages.body: Word games are jogging for your neurons. No running shoes required.

messages.title: Mental Muscle ▲
messages.body: Every word you spell strengthens neural pathways. You're literally building brain power.

messages.title: Neuron News ⬣
messages.body: Your brain has 86 billion neurons. Playing word games thanks every single one of them.

messages.title: Synapse Signal ◌
messages.body: When you learn a word, synapses fire. It's like fireworks, but in your head.

messages.title: Brain Boost ⊞
messages.body: Studies show word games may help maintain cognitive function. Translation: play more Plummet.

messages.title: Age Advantage ⧫
messages.body: Word knowledge actually increases with age. Finally, a superpower that gets better!

messages.title: Bilingual Bonus ◑
messages.body: Know another language? Your word-finding skills have a secret advantage. Use it!

messages.title: Reading Reward ▽
messages.body: Readers have bigger vocabularies. Word gamers have the most fun vocabularies.

messages.title: Writing Wonder ◈
messages.body: Every word you know is a tool for expressing ideas. You're collecting a toolkit.

messages.title: Communication Core ⬡
messages.body: Words are how we share thoughts. Playing with them makes sharing easier.

messages.title: Expression Excellence ⊡
messages.body: The more words you know, the more precisely you can say what you mean. Precision is power.

messages.title: Comprehension Climb ◐
messages.body: Understanding others starts with understanding words. You're building empathy, one letter at a time.

messages.title: Connection Cue ▣
messages.body: Words connect people. When you play, you're practicing connection.

messages.title: Culture Carry ◇
messages.body: Every word carries cultural meaning. You're not just learning vocabulary - you're learning humanity.

messages.title: History Hook ⊕
messages.body: Old words survived because they mattered. When you use them, you're touching history.

messages.title: Future Focus ◎
messages.body: New words are being invented right now. Today's slang is tomorrow's dictionary entry.

messages.title: Evolution Edge ✦
messages.body: Language evolves. Words are born, change, and sometimes die. You're witnessing evolution!

messages.title: Adaptation Angle ◉
messages.body: English absorbs words from everywhere. It's not picky. Neither are word games.

messages.title: Global Glossary ⬢
messages.body: English has words from hundreds of languages. Your keyboard is basically a UN meeting.

messages.title: Local Lingo ◆
messages.body: Regional words are treasures. Wherever you're from, your vocabulary has unique gems.

messages.title: Personal Palette ✧
messages.body: Everyone's vocabulary is unique. Your word collection is as individual as your fingerprint.

messages.title: Style Statement ⊙
messages.body: The words you choose say something about you. Make them count.

messages.title: Voice Value ▲
messages.body: Your written voice is made of word choices. Every game helps you find yours.

messages.title: Creativity Cue ⬣
messages.body: Playing with words is creative thinking. You're an artist whose medium is letters.

messages.title: Innovation Insight ◌
messages.body: New ideas need new combinations of words. Innovation starts with vocabulary.

messages.title: Problem Solving ⊞
messages.body: Finding words in a grid is problem-solving. You're training for life's puzzles.

messages.title: Strategy Session ⧫
messages.body: Should you go for short words or hold out for long ones? Strategy is part of the fun.

messages.title: Decision Drill ◑
messages.body: Every word choice is a micro-decision. You're practicing decisive thinking.

messages.title: Risk & Reward ▽
messages.body: Big words mean big points but bigger risk. Word games teach calculated risks.

messages.title: Patience Practice ◈
messages.body: Sometimes the right word takes time to find. Patience is a skill, and you're building it.

messages.title: Persistence Pays ⬡
messages.body: Stuck? Keep looking. The word is there. Persistence always finds a way.

messages.title: Resilience Reminder ⊡
messages.body: Missed a word? There's always another one. Bouncing back is part of the game.

messages.title: Growth Mindset ◐
messages.body: Every game makes you better, even when you lose. That's the growth mindset in action.

messages.title: Progress Point ▣
messages.body: You know more words today than you did last year. That's progress you can feel proud of.

messages.title: Achievement Angle ◇
messages.body: Every high score started with a first attempt. Keep playing, keep achieving.

messages.title: Milestone Marker ⊕
messages.body: Remember when certain words seemed hard? Now they're easy. Look how far you've come!

messages.title: Improvement Insight ◎
messages.body: Getting faster at finding words? That's your brain rewiring itself for efficiency.

messages.title: Mastery Mode ✦
messages.body: True mastery comes from consistent practice. Every session counts toward expertise.

messages.title: Expert Edge ◉
messages.body: Experts aren't born - they're made through practice. You're on the path right now.

messages.title: Flow State ⬢
messages.body: When words just appear and time disappears - that's flow. Word games can get you there.

messages.title: Zone Zone ◆
messages.body: In the zone? That focused feeling is your brain at peak performance. Ride it!

messages.title: Immersion Insight ✧
messages.body: Deeply engaged in a game? That's immersive learning. The best kind of learning.

messages.title: Enjoyment Engine ⊙
messages.body: Having fun makes learning stick. That smile while playing? It's helping your memory.

messages.title: Relaxation Route ▲
messages.body: Word games can be meditative. Finding words is focusing; focusing is calming.

messages.title: Stress Solution ⬣
messages.body: Need a mental break? Word games are productive pause buttons.

messages.title: Joy Journey ◌
messages.body: The joy of finding a long word is universal. We all share that happy feeling.

messages.title: Satisfaction Signal ⊞
messages.body: That feeling when you nail a tricky word? That's dopamine saying 'well done!'

messages.title: Reward Recognition ⧫
messages.body: Your brain rewards pattern completion. Finding words literally makes you feel good.

messages.title: Celebration Cue ◑
messages.body: Got a high score? Celebrate! Your brain worked hard for that.

messages.title: Victory Vibe ▽
messages.body: Every game ends with a score. Every score is a small victory. You're always winning something.

messages.title: Momentum Maker ◈
messages.body: Good games build momentum. Let today's session fuel tomorrow's greatness.

messages.title: Streak Strength ⬡
messages.body: Playing daily? You're building a streak. Streaks build habits. Habits build skills.

messages.title: Consistency Counts ⊡
messages.body: A little play every day beats a lot once in a while. Consistency is the real cheat code.

messages.title: Routine Rock ◐
messages.body: Word games as part of your routine? You've discovered a life hack.

messages.title: Habit Helper ▣
messages.body: Habits form through repetition. Every session makes playing feel more natural.

messages.title: Daily Dose ◇
messages.body: Your daily dose of vocabulary vitamins is ready. Consume for brain health!

messages.title: Morning Mind ⊕
messages.body: Morning word games wake up your brain better than coffee. Okay, maybe not better. But close!

messages.title: Afternoon Activity ◎
messages.body: Afternoon slump? Word games are a productivity-approved break.

messages.title: Evening Exercise ✦
messages.body: Wind down with words. It's screen time that's actually good for you.

messages.title: Night Notions ◉
messages.body: Late night word sessions hit different. The quiet mind finds words faster.

messages.title: Weekend Words ⬢
messages.body: Weekend plans: relax, recharge, and rack up word game points.

messages.title: Weekday Wisdom ◆
messages.body: Weekdays need word breaks. Your brain deserves a vocabulary vacation.

messages.title: Seasonal Spell ✧
messages.body: Whatever the season, word games are always in style. Evergreen entertainment.

messages.title: Anytime Activity ⊙
messages.body: Waiting in line? Word game. On the bus? Word game. Avoiding responsibilities? ...Word game.

messages.title: Portable Play ▲
messages.body: Your vocabulary goes wherever you go. So does Plummet. Perfect pair.

messages.title: Anywhere Access ⬣
messages.body: Bed, couch, hammock, moon base - word games work everywhere.

messages.title: Social Spell ◌
messages.body: Compare scores with friends. Word games are better together.

messages.title: Competition Cue ⊞
messages.body: A little friendly competition makes everything more fun. Challenge someone!

messages.title: Collaboration Call ⧫
messages.body: Share word tips with friends. Everyone levels up when knowledge is shared.

messages.title: Community Connection ◑
messages.body: Word game players are a community. Welcome to the club!

messages.title: Legacy Letter ▽
messages.body: Every word you master becomes part of your permanent collection. Build your legacy.

messages.title: Future Find ◈
messages.body: The words you learn today will serve you tomorrow. You're investing in future you.

messages.title: Timeless Treasure ⬡
messages.body: Words never go out of style. You're collecting timeless treasures.

messages.title: Endless Exploration ⊡
messages.body: English has over a million words. You'll never run out of new ones to find!

messages.title: Infinite Interest ◐
messages.body: There's always another word, another game, another score to beat. The fun never ends.

messages.title: Eternal Edge ▣
messages.body: Word skills last a lifetime. Every game is an investment in permanent capability.

messages.title: Forever Fun ◇
messages.body: Word games have been played for centuries. You're part of an ancient tradition.

messages.title: Ageless Appeal ⊕
messages.body: 5 or 95, word games work for everyone. Language is the universal game.

messages.title: Universal Understanding ◎
messages.body: Words bridge gaps between people. Every word you know helps you understand more.

messages.title: Common Ground ✦
messages.body: Word games are common ground. Anywhere in the world, people play with words.

messages.title: Shared Space ◉
messages.body: When you play, you're sharing in something millions of others enjoy. Connection through letters.

messages.title: Collective Creation ⬢
messages.body: Language belongs to all of us. Playing with words is participating in its evolution.

messages.title: Human Heritage ◆
messages.body: Words are humanity's greatest invention. You're playing with the building blocks of civilization.

messages.title: Mind Museum ✧
messages.body: Your vocabulary is a museum of concepts. Every word is an exhibit worth visiting.

messages.title: Mental Map ⊙
messages.body: Words map out meaning in your mind. The more words, the more detailed your map.

messages.title: Thought Tool ▲
messages.body: We think in words. Better vocabulary = better thinking. It's that simple.

messages.title: Idea Inventory ⬣
messages.body: Every word is an idea you can access. You're building an inventory of thoughts.

messages.title: Concept Collection ◌
messages.body: Collecting words is collecting concepts. You're a curator of meaning.

messages.title: Knowledge Key ⊞
messages.body: Words unlock knowledge. The more keys you have, the more doors you can open.

messages.title: Learning Ladder ⧫
messages.body: Every word is a rung on the ladder of learning. Keep climbing!

messages.title: Wisdom Well ◑
messages.body: Words are drops in the well of wisdom. Draw from it whenever you need.

messages.title: Discovery Drive ▽
messages.body: There's a word for almost everything. Discovery never stops when you love language.

messages.title: Wonder Word ◈
messages.body: Ever find a word and think 'there's a word for that!'? That wonder is the heart of language.

messages.title: Curiosity Call ⬡
messages.body: Curious about words? Good. Curiosity is the engine of vocabulary growth.

messages.title: Question Quest ⊡
messages.body: Why is 'abbreviation' such a long word? Questions about language lead to deeper understanding.

messages.title: Answer Avenue ◐
messages.body: Every question about words has an answer. Usually an interesting one. Keep asking!

messages.title: Exploration Expedition ▣
messages.body: Think of every game as an expedition into the word wilderness. Adventure awaits!

messages.title: Journey Joy ◇
messages.body: The journey through language never ends. That's what makes it joyful.

messages.title: Path Point ⊕
messages.body: Every word game is a step on the path of mastery. You're making progress right now.

messages.title: Road Reward ◎
messages.body: The road to vocabulary mastery is paved with fun games. Enjoy the trip!

messages.title: Destination Delight ✦
messages.body: Where does the word road lead? Wherever you want to go. Words take you there.

messages.title: Horizon Hope ◉
messages.body: There's always more vocabulary beyond the horizon. Keep exploring!

messages.title: Summit Signal ⬢
messages.body: Every high score is a summit reached. Many more peaks await. Keep climbing!

messages.title: Peak Performance ◆
messages.body: Your peak performance is always one game away. This could be the one!

messages.title: Best Bet ✧
messages.body: Betting on yourself to improve? That's always a winning bet. Play on!

messages.title: Champion Choice ⊙
messages.body: Champions are made one game at a time. You're building championship material.

messages.title: Winner Wisdom ▲
messages.body: Winners keep playing. Losers give up. You're reading this, so... winner!

messages.title: Victory Vision ⬣
messages.body: Can you see your next high score? Vision it, then achieve it.

messages.title: Success Story ◌
messages.body: Your word game success story is being written right now. Keep adding chapters!

messages.title: Triumph Trail ⊞
messages.body: Every game leaves a trail of triumphs. Look back and see how far you've come.

messages.title: Glory Game ⧫
messages.body: There's glory in every game well played. Today's session is tomorrow's glory.

messages.title: Legend Level ◑
messages.body: Legends are made through legendary effort. Every word spelled is part of your legend.

messages.title: Tomorrow's Triumph ▽
messages.body: Today's practice is tomorrow's performance. You're rehearsing for greatness.

messages.title: Star Speller ◈
messages.body: You're a star. Stars shine brightest when they play word games. Scientific fact.

messages.title: Shine On ⬡
messages.body: Your vocabulary shines. Let it light up the leaderboard!

messages.title: Bright Beginning ⊡
messages.body: Every great word game career started with a single letter. Yours is already ahead!

messages.title: Final Flourish ◐
messages.body: This message ends, but your word journey continues. See you on the board!

messages.title: Alphabet Architect ◈
messages.body: You don't just find words - you build them. Letter by letter, you're an architect.

messages.title: Lexical Legend ⬡
messages.body: Some people play games. You craft linguistic masterpieces. There's a difference.

messages.title: Syllable Sculptor ◇
messages.body: Every word you find is a sculpture carved from the chaos of letters. Art!

messages.title: Verbal Virtuoso ⊕
messages.body: A virtuoso makes the difficult look easy. That's you with words.

messages.title: Phonetic Philosopher ◎
messages.body: What is a word? A sound with meaning. Deep thoughts for a word game.

messages.title: Semantic Sage ✦
messages.body: You understand meaning at a deeper level. That's sage-level vocabulary wisdom.

messages.title: Grammar Guardian ◉
messages.body: Someone has to protect the language. Might as well be you, word warrior.

messages.title: Diction Detective ⬢
messages.body: You investigate words like a detective. No letter escapes your scrutiny.

messages.title: Prose Pioneer ◆
messages.body: Pioneers explore new territory. Your territory? The endless frontier of vocabulary.

messages.title: Rhetoric Ranger ✧
messages.body: Rangers patrol the wilderness. You patrol the wild world of words.

messages.title: Syntax Sentinel ⊙
messages.body: Standing guard over word order since... well, since you started playing.

messages.title: Vocabulary Viking ▲
messages.body: You raid the dictionary with the ferocity of a Viking. Claim those words!

messages.title: Letter Luminary ⬣
messages.body: A luminary lights the way. Your vocabulary illuminates possibilities.

messages.title: Word Wanderer ◌
messages.body: Not all who wander are lost. Some are just looking for seven-letter words.

messages.title: Alphabet Alchemist ⊞
messages.body: Turning random letters into golden words. That's alchemy, that is.

messages.title: Spelling Samurai ⧫
messages.body: Swift, precise, deadly accurate. Your spelling cuts through confusion.

messages.title: Linguistic Lion ◑
messages.body: The lion is king of the jungle. You're king of the letter jungle.

messages.title: Verbal Voyager ▽
messages.body: Every game is a voyage to new vocabulary shores. Bon voyage!

messages.title: Diction Diplomat ◈
messages.body: You negotiate between letters with the skill of a seasoned diplomat.

messages.title: Etymology Explorer ⬡
messages.body: Where do words come from? Explorers like you find out, one game at a time.

messages.title: Morphology Master ⊡
messages.body: You understand how words are built. That's morphology mastery right there.

messages.title: Phonology Phenom ◐
messages.body: Sound patterns? You've got them figured out. Phenomenal phonology skills.

messages.title: Pragmatics Pro ▣
messages.body: Using words in context? That's pragmatics. You're a professional at it.

messages.title: Lexeme Leader ◇
messages.body: Lexemes are the building blocks of vocabulary. You lead them into formation.

messages.title: Grapheme Guru ⊕
messages.body: Letters on screen bow to your grapheme wisdom. Guru status achieved.

messages.title: Morpheme Maestro ◎
messages.body: Conducting the orchestra of word parts. Every morpheme plays its role.

messages.title: Allomorph Ace ✦
messages.body: Same morpheme, different forms. You handle allomorphs like an ace.

messages.title: Affix Aficionado ◉
messages.body: Prefixes before, suffixes after. You affix with the passion of an aficionado.

messages.title: Derivation Deity ⬢
messages.body: Deriving new words from old? That's basically divine power. Word deity status.

messages.title: Inflection Icon ◆
messages.body: Conjugations, declensions, inflections - you handle them all iconically.

messages.title: Compound Commander ✧
messages.body: Combining words into compounds? You command that operation flawlessly.

messages.title: Blend Boss ⊙
messages.body: Brunch, smog, motel - blends are word magic. You're the boss of blends.

messages.title: Clipping Champion ▲
messages.body: Phone from telephone, ad from advertisement. Clipping champion right here.

messages.title: Acronym Artist ⬣
messages.body: NASA, RADAR, SCUBA - you appreciate the art of the acronym.

messages.title: Backformation Boss ◌
messages.body: Editor from edit? That's backformation. You understand the reverse engineering.

messages.title: Coinage Captain ⊞
messages.body: New words need to be coined. Maybe you'll mint the next one!

messages.title: Borrowing Buff ⧫
messages.body: English borrows from everywhere. You appreciate this linguistic generosity.

messages.title: Calque Connoisseur ◑
messages.body: Loan translations are calques. 'Skyscraper' inspired 'gratte-ciel.' Fancy, right?

messages.title: Semantic Shift Scholar ▽
messages.body: Words change meaning over time. 'Awful' used to mean 'awe-inspiring.' True story.

messages.title: Amelioration Aware ◈
messages.body: 'Knight' once meant 'boy' or 'servant.' Words can upgrade. Amelioration!

messages.title: Pejoration Ponderer ⬡
messages.body: 'Villain' once meant 'farm worker.' Words can downgrade. That's pejoration.

messages.title: Broadening Brain ⊡
messages.body: 'Dog' used to mean one specific breed. Now it's all dogs. Semantic broadening!

messages.title: Narrowing Ninja ◐
messages.body: 'Meat' once meant all food. Now just animal flesh. Semantic narrowing ninja!

messages.title: Metaphor Maven ▣
messages.body: Metaphors make language colorful. You paint with words, maven.

messages.title: Metonymy Master ◇
messages.body: 'The White House said...' That's metonymy. You master these substitutions.

messages.title: Synecdoche Savant ⊕
messages.body: 'All hands on deck!' Hands for sailors - that's synecdoche. Savant-level knowledge.

messages.title: Irony Interpreter ◎
messages.body: Saying the opposite of what you mean? Irony. You interpret it expertly.

messages.title: Litotes Lover ✦
messages.body: 'Not bad' meaning 'good' - that's litotes. Understatement appreciation unlocked.

messages.title: Hyperbole Hero ◉
messages.body: Exaggeration for effect? You're literally the best at understanding hyperbole. See?

messages.title: Euphemism Expert ⬢
messages.body: Saying things nicely? 'Passed away' instead of 'died.' Euphemism expertise.

messages.title: Dysphemism Devotee ◆
messages.body: The opposite of euphemism - being blunt. You appreciate both styles.

messages.title: Periphrasis Pro ✧
messages.body: Using many words when few would do. Why say 'king' when 'crowned head' exists?

messages.title: Pleonasm Pal ⊙
messages.body: 'Free gift' - the gift is already free! Pleonasm is redundant redundancy.

messages.title: Tautology Thinker ▲
messages.body: 'It is what it is.' Tautology states the obvious. Now you know what you know.

messages.title: Zeugma Zealot ⬣
messages.body: 'She broke his car and his heart.' One verb, two objects. Zeugma zealotry!

messages.title: Chiasmus Champion ◌
messages.body: 'Ask not what your country can do...' Reversed structures. Chiasmus champion!

messages.title: Anaphora Admirer ⊞
messages.body: Repetition at the start: 'I have a dream...' repeated. Anaphora appreciation.

messages.title: Epistrophe Expert ⧫
messages.body: Repetition at the end: 'government of the people, by the people, for the people.'

messages.title: Anadiplosis Ace ◑
messages.body: Ending one clause and starting the next with the same word. Word linking ace!

messages.title: Parallelism Partner ▽
messages.body: Same structure, different content. Parallelism makes prose powerful. Partner up!

messages.title: Antithesis Appreciator ◈
messages.body: Contrasting ideas in parallel: 'It was the best of times, worst of times.'

messages.title: Asyndeton Aware ⬡
messages.body: No conjunctions: 'I came, I saw, I conquered.' Fast and punchy. Asyndeton!

messages.title: Polysyndeton Ponderer ⊡
messages.body: Many conjunctions: 'And this and that and more.' Deliberate and emphatic.

messages.title: Ellipsis Enthusiast ◐
messages.body: Missing words that are understood: 'I can [do it] and will [do it].' Ellipsis!

messages.title: Parenthesis Pioneer ▣
messages.body: Inserting extra info (like this) into sentences. Parenthesis pioneering!

messages.title: Apposition Authority ◇
messages.body: 'My friend, the doctor, helped.' Extra description. Apposition authority.

messages.title: Hendiadys Helper ⊕
messages.body: 'Nice and warm' instead of 'nicely warm.' Two words for one idea. Hendiadys!

messages.title: Syllepsis Savvy ◎
messages.body: One word, two meanings: 'She lowered her standards and her neckline.'

messages.title: Anacoluthon Adventurer ✦
messages.body: Starting a sentence one way, finishing another. Grammatical plot twist!

messages.title: Enallage Expert ◉
messages.body: Deliberate grammar 'mistakes' for effect. Breaking rules knowingly. Expert move.

messages.title: Anthimeria Ace ⬢
messages.body: Using a word as a different part of speech: 'Google it.' Verbing nouns since forever.

messages.title: Catachresis Creator ◆
messages.body: Mixed metaphors on purpose. Sometimes breaking rules creates new meaning.

messages.title: Malapropism Monitor ✧
messages.body: Wrong word, similar sound: 'For all intensive purposes.' That's 'intents and purposes!'

messages.title: Eggcorn Expert ⊙
messages.body: 'Old-timers disease' instead of 'Alzheimer's.' Wrong but logical. Eggcorn expertise.

messages.title: Spoonerism Spotter ▲
messages.body: 'A blushing crow' instead of 'a crushing blow.' Letter swap humor!

messages.title: Mondegreen Maven ⬣
messages.body: Misheard lyrics: 'Excuse me while I kiss this guy.' It's 'kiss the sky!' Maven status.

messages.title: Folk Etymology Fan ◌
messages.body: 'Chaise lounge' from 'chaise longue.' People reshape words to make sense.

messages.title: Rebracketing Recognizer ⊞
messages.body: 'A napron' became 'an apron.' Word boundaries shift over time. Recognized!

messages.title: Haplology Helper ⧫
messages.body: 'Probly' instead of 'probably.' Dropping similar syllables. Happens naturally.

messages.title: Epenthesis Explorer ◑
messages.body: Adding sounds: 'Athalete' instead of 'athlete.' Sound insertion explored!

messages.title: Metathesis Master ▽
messages.body: Sound switching: 'Aks' instead of 'ask.' Metathesis happens more than you think.

messages.title: Assimilation Aware ◈
messages.body: 'Impolite' not 'inpolite' - sounds influence their neighbors. Assimilation awareness!

messages.title: Dissimilation Detective ⬡
messages.body: When similar sounds become different to avoid confusion. Linguistic adjustment!

messages.title: Liaison Learner ⊡
messages.body: Linking sounds between words: 'Tuna-r-in-a-can.' Smooth speech secrets.

messages.title: Elision Enthusiast ◐
messages.body: Dropping sounds: 'Prob'ly' instead of 'probably.' Elision makes speech flow.

messages.title: Stress Strategist ▣
messages.body: REcord vs reCORD - stress changes meaning. Strategic stress mastery!

messages.title: Intonation Insider ◇
messages.body: Rising tone for questions, falling for statements. You're inside intonation's secrets.

messages.title: Pitch Pattern Pro ⊕
messages.body: Melody in speech? That's pitch patterns. You hear the music in words.

messages.title: Rhythm Recognition ◎
messages.body: English has a beat: stressed and unstressed syllables. Rhythm recognized!

messages.title: Tempo Tracker ✦
messages.body: Fast speech, slow speech - tempo changes meaning and mood. Tracked!

messages.title: Pause Power ◉
messages.body: A well-placed pause... creates meaning. Powerful punctuation in speech.

messages.title: Register Ranger ⬢
messages.body: Formal, informal, casual, frozen - different situations, different registers.

messages.title: Code-Switching Coder ◆
messages.body: Switching between languages or dialects? That's code-switching. Linguistic flexibility!

messages.title: Diglossia Detective ✧
messages.body: Two varieties of a language for different purposes. Diglossia detected!

messages.title: Pidgin Pioneer ⊙
messages.body: Simplified contact languages between groups. Pidgins pave communication paths.

messages.title: Creole Curator ▲
messages.body: When pidgins become native languages - creoles! Full languages from contact.

messages.title: Lingua Franca Fan ⬣
messages.body: A common language between different speakers. English is today's lingua franca!

messages.title: Jargon Judge ◌
messages.body: Every field has specialized vocabulary. You judge jargon like a pro.

messages.title: Argot Appreciator ⊞
messages.body: Secret language of specific groups. Argot appreciation unlocked.

messages.title: Cant Connoisseur ⧫
messages.body: Language meant to exclude outsiders. Historical and fascinating. Connoisseur level.

messages.title: Shibboleth Spotter ◑
messages.body: Words that reveal your origin or group. A pronunciation can be a password.

messages.title: Isogloss Investigator ▽
messages.body: Lines on maps where language features change. Geographic linguistics!

messages.title: Dialect Distinction ◈
messages.body: Regional varieties are treasures, not errors. Dialect distinction matters.

messages.title: Sociolect Scholar ⬡
messages.body: Language varies by social group too. You're scholarly about sociolects.

messages.title: Idiolect Identifier ⊡
messages.body: Your personal language variety - your idiolect. Everyone has a unique one!

messages.title: Chronolect Champion ◐
messages.body: Language of different age groups. Generational vocabulary champion!

messages.title: Ethnolect Expert ▣
messages.body: Language varieties associated with ethnic groups. Linguistic diversity expert.

messages.title: Genderlect Guide ◇
messages.body: Language patterns associated with gender. Guiding through linguistic variety.

messages.title: Technolect Tracker ⊕
messages.body: Tech creates new words constantly. You track the technolect evolution.

messages.title: Netspeak Navigator ◎
messages.body: LOL, BRB, TTYL - internet language has its own rules. Navigate the netspeak!

messages.title: Emoji Evaluator ✦
messages.body: Are emojis language? They communicate meaning. You evaluate thoughtfully.

messages.title: Hashtag Historian ◉
messages.body: From pound sign to cultural phenomenon. Hashtag history is wild.

messages.title: Meme Meaning ⬢
messages.body: Memes spread and evolve like words do. Meaning in image form.

messages.title: GIF Grammar ◆
messages.body: Is it GIF or JIF? Either way, animated images communicate. GIF grammar!

messages.title: Autocorrect Awareness ✧
messages.body: Autocorrect creates new 'words' daily. Ducking funny, right?

messages.title: Predictive Peculiarity ⊙
messages.body: Predictive text knows you. Sometimes too well. Peculiarly personal algorithms.

messages.title: Voice Recognition Vision ▲
messages.body: Machines understanding speech is revolutionary. Voice recognition is the future.

messages.title: Translation Transformation ⬣
messages.body: From one language to another - translation transforms meaning across borders.

messages.title: Interpretation Independence ◌
messages.body: Simultaneous interpretation in real-time. Linguists with superhuman skills.

messages.title: Localization Logic ⊞
messages.body: Adapting content for different cultures. More than translation - localization!

messages.title: Globalization Grammar ⧫
messages.body: English spreads globally, but changes locally. Globalization's grammar.

messages.title: Preservation Priority ◑
messages.body: Endangered languages need preservation. Every word lost is history lost.

messages.title: Revival Recognition ▽
messages.body: Hebrew was revived! Languages can come back. Revival is possible.

messages.title: Documentation Drive ◈
messages.body: Linguists race to document endangered languages. Every word matters.

messages.title: Diversity Defender ⬡
messages.body: 7,000+ languages exist. You defend linguistic diversity by loving language.

messages.title: Polyglot Potential ⊡
messages.body: Speaking multiple languages? That's polyglot power. Maybe you'll learn more!

messages.title: Monolingual Magic ◐
messages.body: One language deeply known is still magical. Quality over quantity can shine.

messages.title: Bilingual Brain ▣
messages.body: Bilingual brains work differently. If you speak two, you have a superpower.

messages.title: Multilingual Mindset ◇
messages.body: The more languages, the more worldviews. Multilingual minds see more.

messages.title: Language Learner ⊕
messages.body: Still learning? Perfect. Language learning never really ends. Learner forever.

messages.title: Acquisition Amazement ◎
messages.body: Children acquire language naturally. It's amazing when you think about it.

messages.title: Critical Period Curiosity ✦
messages.body: Is there a best age to learn languages? Curious question, complex answer.

messages.title: Input Insight ◉
messages.body: Language needs input to grow. Reading, listening, playing - it all feeds fluency.

messages.title: Output Opportunity ⬢
messages.body: Speaking and writing practice matters too. Output creates opportunity.

messages.title: Interaction Importance ◆
messages.body: Real communication beats drills. Interaction is where language lives.

messages.title: Immersion Impact ✧
messages.body: Surround yourself with a language and watch progress accelerate. Immersion impact!

messages.title: Motivation Matters ⊙
messages.body: Why do you play word games? Motivation drives learning. Keep yours strong.

messages.title: Aptitude Angle ▲
messages.body: Some have natural language aptitude. But practice beats talent that doesn't try.

messages.title: Strategy Strength ⬣
messages.body: Good learning strategies multiply effort. Work smarter with words.

messages.title: Feedback Focus ◌
messages.body: Feedback helps you improve. Every game score is feedback. Focus on it!

messages.title: Error Embrace ⊞
messages.body: Mistakes are learning opportunities. Embrace errors as teachers.

messages.title: Fossilization Fight ⧫
messages.body: Don't let mistakes become permanent! Keep improving, keep fighting fossilization.

messages.title: Interlanguage Interest ◑
messages.body: Your developing language system is unique. Interlanguage is interesting!

messages.title: Transfer Triumph ▽
messages.body: Skills from one language help another. Positive transfer is a triumph.

messages.title: Interference Insight ◈
messages.body: Sometimes old habits interfere with new learning. Insight helps overcome.

messages.title: Cognate Connection ⬡
messages.body: Words similar across languages: night/nacht/nuit/notte. Cognate connections!

messages.title: False Friend Finder ⊡
messages.body: 'Embarazada' means pregnant, not embarrassed! False friends deceive. Find them first!

messages.title: Root Recognition ◐
messages.body: Latin and Greek roots appear everywhere. Recognize roots, unlock words.

messages.title: Prefix Proficiency ▣
messages.body: Pre-, un-, re-, dis- modify meaning. Prefix proficiency unlocks vocabulary.

messages.title: Suffix Savvy ◇
messages.body: -tion, -ness, -able, -ful change words. Suffix savvy expands your options.

messages.title: Combining Forms ⊕
messages.body: Bio-, -logy, tele-, -graph combine into thousands of words. Form combinations!

messages.title: Word Family Fun ◎
messages.body: Create, creation, creative, creator - word families expand together.

messages.title: Collocation Catch ✦
messages.body: Words that go together: 'make a decision' not 'do a decision.' Catch collocations!

messages.title: Chunk Champion ◉
messages.body: Learn phrases as chunks: 'by the way,' 'in other words.' Champion chunking!

messages.title: Frequency Favorite ⬢
messages.body: The most frequent words matter most. You already know the favorites!

messages.title: Coverage Calculation ◆
messages.body: 2,000 words = 90% of everyday speech. Coverage calculated. You're covered!

messages.title: Depth vs Breadth ✧
messages.body: Know many words shallowly or few words deeply? Both matter. Balance them.

messages.title: Active vs Passive ⊙
messages.body: Words you use vs words you understand. Passive vocabulary is always bigger.

messages.title: Receptive Range ▲
messages.body: What you can understand when reading and listening. Your receptive range grows!

messages.title: Productive Power ⬣
messages.body: What you can actually produce when speaking and writing. Productive power up!

messages.title: Threshold Theory ◌
messages.body: Need enough vocabulary to read comfortably. You're past the threshold!

messages.title: Incidental Learning ⊞
messages.body: Learning words without trying - from context. Word games are incidental genius.

messages.title: Intentional Improvement ⧫
messages.body: Deliberately learning words works too. Your intention drives improvement.

messages.title: Spaced Repetition ◑
messages.body: Review words at increasing intervals. Spaced repetition cements memory.

messages.title: Context Clues ▽
messages.body: Unknown word? Look around it. Context gives clues to meaning.

messages.title: Dictionary Dive ◈
messages.body: When in doubt, look it up. Dictionary dives deepen understanding.

messages.title: Thesaurus Thrill ⬡
messages.body: Synonyms expand expression. The thesaurus is thrilling territory.

messages.title: Concordance Quest ⊡
messages.body: How is a word actually used? Concordances show real examples. Quest on!

messages.title: Corpus Curiosity ◐
messages.body: Language corpora show how words behave in the wild. Curious and useful!

messages.title: Etymology Exploration ▣
messages.body: Word histories reveal meanings. Etymology exploration enriches understanding.

messages.title: Semantic Memory ◇
messages.body: Your brain stores word meanings in networks. Semantic memory strengthens with play.

messages.title: Mental Lexicon ⊕
messages.body: Your personal internal dictionary. Your mental lexicon grows with every game.

messages.title: Spreading Activation ◎
messages.body: Think 'cat,' brain activates 'pet,' 'fur,' 'meow.' Spreading activation helps retrieval.

messages.title: Priming Power ✦
messages.body: Seeing one word makes related words easier to recognize. Priming powers play!

messages.title: Retrieval Practice ◉
messages.body: Pulling words from memory strengthens them. Word games are retrieval practice!

messages.title: Encoding Enhancement ⬢
messages.body: How you learn affects how you remember. Deep processing enhances encoding.

messages.title: Elaboration Effect ◆
messages.body: Connect new words to what you know. Elaboration makes learning stick.

messages.title: Imagery Impact ✧
messages.body: Visualize words and they stick better. Mental images have impact.

messages.title: Association Advantage ⊙
messages.body: Link words to personal experiences. Associations give you an advantage.

messages.title: Mnemonic Method ▲
messages.body: Memory tricks help sticky learning. There's a mnemonic for that!

messages.title: Keyword Connection ⬣
messages.body: Link foreign words to familiar sounds. The keyword method connects!

messages.title: Peg System ◌
messages.body: One is a bun, two is a shoe... peg systems hang words on numbers.

messages.title: Memory Palace ⊞
messages.body: Place words in imaginary rooms. Walk through to remember. Palace method!

messages.title: Acronym Aid ⧫
messages.body: HOMES for Great Lakes, ROY G BIV for colors. Acronyms aid memory.

messages.title: Song String ◑
messages.body: Put words to music and they stick. The ABC song works. So can others.

messages.title: Rhyme Reason ▽
messages.body: Rhymes are memorable: 'i before e except after c.' There's method in rhyme.

messages.title: Story Strength ◈
messages.body: Weave vocabulary into stories. Narrative gives words staying power.

messages.title: Drawing Devices ⬡
messages.body: Sketch word meanings. Visual memory complements verbal. Drawing helps!

messages.title: Acting Action ⊡
messages.body: Act out words physically. Movement memory adds another layer.

messages.title: Sensory Support ◐
messages.body: Engage multiple senses. See it, say it, hear it, write it. Sensory support!

messages.title: Emotional Encoding ▣
messages.body: Emotional words stick better. Feel the words, remember them longer.

messages.title: Novelty Note ◇
messages.body: Unusual words get noticed. Novelty captures attention and memory.

messages.title: Relevance Rule ⊕
messages.body: Personally relevant words stick. Find the relevance, find the memory.

messages.title: Goal Guidance ◎
messages.body: Learning with a goal in mind is more effective. Goals guide progress.

messages.title: Progress Pleasure ✦
messages.body: Seeing improvement feels good. That pleasure motivates more learning.

messages.title: Challenge Calibration ◉
messages.body: Not too easy, not too hard. Calibrated challenge keeps you in flow.

messages.title: Autonomy Advantage ⬢
messages.body: Choosing what to learn increases motivation. Autonomy advantages abound.
